pathA: PUBLIC PATH (README quickstart) — breaks at step 2, in a worse-than-404 way. README.md:23/215/297 says `dotnet add package Koan.Core Koan.Web Koan.Data.Connector.Sqlite`. Verified against nuget.org (2026-06-10): (1) `Koan.Core` and `Koan.Web` EXIST unprefixed at v0.5.2 only (published by 'Sylin Labs', ~934 downloads, projectUrl points to dead org github.com/sylin-labs/Koan-framework) — so the first two installs SILENTLY SUCCEED with a 13-months-stale package missing most documented APIs. (2) `Koan.Data.Connector.Sqlite` does NOT exist unprefixed → NU1101 hard failure on the documented one-liner ( `Koan.Data.Sqlite` exists at 0.5.2 under the pre-rename ID). (3) The real current packages are `Sylin.Koan.*` at 0.8.0 (157 package IDs, net10.0, e.g. Sylin.Koan.Data.Connector.Sqlite 0.8.0) — but NO user-facing doc ever says 'Sylin.'; the prefix appears only in internal decisions (DX-0036, ARCH-0010, ARCH-0085) and packaging/README.md ('published under Sylin.Koan.*'). Repo source is 0.17.x (NBGV), so even the correct Sylin 0.8.0 install is ~9 versions behind the docs describing it. (4) README's 'Try the quickstart': `git clone https://github.com/koan-framework/quickstart` → verified 404; org `koan-framework` does not exist (repo lives at sylin-org); `koan-framework/enterprise-sample` also 404. (5) README badge says v0.6.3 — a version never published to nuget.org (gap: 0.5.2 → 0.8.0); .claude/skills/quickstart/SKILL.md even pins `--version 0.6.3`, which cannot resolve. (6) README §3/§4 advertise phantom APIs for the install path: `Flow.OnUpdate<T>` (Koan.Flow.Core contains only TECHNICAL.md — no code), `Todo.SemanticSearch(...)` as an entity static (actual API is plain static helper `EntityEmbeddingExtensions.SemanticSearch<TEntity>(query,...)` in Koan.Data.AI — the README call shape doesn't compile), `koan export compose` (condemned Orchestration CLI per ARCH-0077), and `dotnet add package Koan.Messaging.InMemory` (no such project; only src/Connectors/Messaging/RabbitMq exists). NEWCOMER EXPERIENCE: two stale packages install silently, third errors NU1101; searching nuget.org for 'Koan' surfaces a confusing dual namespace (Koan.* @0.5.2 vs Sylin.Koan.* @0.8.0, both pointing at a renamed GitHub org); the advertised quickstart repo 404s. Probability an outsider reaches a running hello-CRUD from the README alone: near zero. local-feed/ contains only 20 Sylin.* 0.8.2 nupkgs (abstractions + auth connectors — no Core-feeding Web/Sqlite set) and there is no nuget.config wiring it, so it doesn't rescue the path either.
pathB: REPO-CLONE PATH — the only viable entry, but the documented sequence is misdirection layered on a working core. (1) docs/getting-started/quickstart.md and guide.md are both `status: deprecated` tombstones redirecting to overview.md (DX-0041), which re-states the broken Path-A package commands — there is NO documented 'clone the repo, build Koan.sln, run a sample' sequence anywhere; the actually-viable path (open Koan.sln, F5 a sample) is undocumented. overview.md also teaches Stage-3 `Flow.OnUpdate` (pillar gutted), `Koan.Messaging.InMemory` (nonexistent), and pins framework_version v0.6.3. (2) No start.bat at repo root; per-sample start.bat exists (S0, S1 incl. start-compose/start-docker variants, S5, S10, S14, S16) consistent with samples/README. (3) Koan.sln contains exactly 13 sample projects: S0, S1, S3.Mq.Sample, S5.Recs, S8.Canon.Api+Shared, S10, S14, S16(API+MCP), S18.Prism, g1c1.GardenCoop — the Stage-1 'works' set (S0/S1/S10/S14) is all in-sln; the broken ones (S6.SnapVault, S8.PolyglotShop) plus S7.Meridian, S8.Location, S9.Location are NOT in the sln, so `dotnet build Koan.sln` shields the newcomer until they wander into a directory (S8.PolyglotShop references zero-consumer experimental projects Koan.ServiceMesh + Koan.Service.Translation; S6.SnapVault references Media/Storage strata). Note CATALOG says 'Run: dotnet run from S8.Canon directory' but the root S8.Canon.csproj is not in the sln (only Api/Shared are). (4) CATALOG.md (last updated 2025-10-16, v0.6.3) and samples/README advertise FOUR phantom samples as '🔨 Building/In Development Phase 2-5, weeks 3-21': S3.NotifyHub, S4.DevHub, S6.MediaHub, S9.OrderFlow — none exist under those names ~8 months later; the capability matrix's 'Best Example' cells for the entire Messaging, Secrets, Media, and CQRS rows point exclusively at these phantoms (S3*, S4*, S6*, S9*), and cites S7 (TechDocs) for authorization — itself a ghost. (5) Inverse drift: real, substantial samples are invisible — S6.SnapVault, S7.Meridian (full app w/ ARCHITECTURE.md), S8.Location, S8.PolyglotShop, S9.Location absent from both README index and CATALOG; S18.Prism is in README's index but missing from CATALOG; S3.Mq.Sample (in sln, builds) is documented nowhere. (6) Five top-level ghost directories contain ONLY bin/obj artifacts of 'archived' samples: S4.Web, S7.TechDocs, S12.MedTrials.Core, S12.MedTrials.McpService, S13.DocMind.Tools — so samples/ shows ~23 dirs of which 5 are empty husks, ~5 are undocumented, 4 documented ones are phantoms. The recommended ladder itself (S0 → S1 → S10, then S5) is real and runnable — the best-maintained artifact in the whole onboarding surface — but the newcomer must ignore the catalog's status column, port table (lists S7 as 'Reserved for future' while two S7 dirs sit on disk), and capability matrix to find it.

helloCrud: Entity<TSelf> self-typed active-record base (string Id, GUID v7 auto-generation)
Entity statics: Get/All/Query/Save/Delete/Batch/AllStream — and the ambient AppHost.Current service-locator that makes them work (invisible until first console app or test breaks)
EntityController<TEntity> base class + its RFC-flavored REST conventions (upsert POST, pagination Link headers, sets)
AddKoan() single-call bootstrap + KoanAutoRegistrar/'Reference = Intent' auto-discovery (why Program.cs registers nothing)
Provider election: referenced adapter wins by priority (S1 references BOTH Json and Sqlite connectors; which one wins requires reading the boot report), zero-config connection defaults (./data dir)
Auto schema creation ('magic') and its environment gate Koan:AllowMagicInProduction — surfaces on first non-Development run
Boot report / 'Koan:modules' provenance output — the first thing printed, and the designated debugging surface

Opinionated implicit web pipeline via IStartupFilter: auto-mapped controllers, /api/health, static files, secure headers — and Newtonsoft.Json camelCase/ignore-nulls serialization instead of System.Text.Json (breaks STJ-attribute assumptions)
realApp: EntityContext.Partition ambient partition routing (DATA-0077/ARCH-0069 import pipeline: 26 usages in S5)
[Embedding(Properties, Async, RateLimitPerMinute, MaxTokens, Version, WarnOnTruncation)] attribute-driven embedding pipeline + framework-managed EmbeddingState<T> (ARCH-0070)
Vector<TEntity> static facade: IsAvailable/Search/GetEmbeddings/Stats + capability probing and graceful degradation when Weaviate is absent
Multi-adapter coexistence and routing: Mongo (documents) + Weaviate (vectors) + Ollama (AI) elected purely by project reference
Koan.Scheduling: SchedulingOptions, Jobs dictionary, OnStartup, ReadinessGate
[KoanApp] orchestration manifest + KoanRequiresOrchestrationGenerator (rides the ARCH-0077-condemned stack)
ADR literacy: flagship sample code is annotated by ADR number (ARCH-0069/0070, AI-0020) — full comprehension requires chasing docs/decisions/

Doctrine exceptions: S5's own Program.cs manually registers framework service IRelationshipMetadata ('Explicitly register parent relationship metadata service') that Koan.Data.Core already TryAdds — the flagship violates the 'never manually register framework services' rule the bootstrap skill calls mandatory
conceptLoad.koanSaves: Versus ASP.NET+EF for hello-CRUD: eliminates DbContext/DbSet authoring, AddDbContext+connection-string config, migrations/EnsureCreated, hand-written CRUD actions (5 endpoints), MapControllers/health-check wiring, and ID generation — ~12-line app vs ~60-80 lines + migration workflow; concept COUNT is roughly equal (~8 vs ~9) but Koan's lines-of-ceremony is dramatically lower. Versus ASP.NET for the S5-class app the savings are genuinely large: vector-DB client code, embedding generation/state tracking/rate limiting, polyglot persistence routing, dev-auth loop, scheduler wiring (Hangfire/Quartz-equivalent), and capability fallback collapse into attributes and statics — plausibly thousands of lines of bespoke integration.
conceptLoad.koanAdds: ~8 proprietary concepts for hello-CRUD and ~16 more for a real app (~24 total for S5-level fluency) that have zero transfer value and no community: auto-registration magic and provider election that are invisible until they misbehave (the designated remedy — reading boot reports — is itself a concept), an ambient service-locator (AppHost.Current) contradicting standard DI mental models, a non-default JSON serializer, environment-gated DDL magic, partition/set routing semantics, and an ADR-numbered vocabulary. The ASP.NET path's equal-or-larger concept count is all transferable, exhaustively documented, and AI-assistant-trainable; Koan's is documented only by this repo, where the entry docs misstate package IDs, versions, and at least three APIs (Flow.OnUpdate, entity-static SemanticSearch, Koan.Messaging.InMemory).

bootstrapVariants: VARIANT 1 — console one-liner: services.StartKoan() (S0.ConsoleJsonRepo; defined in Koan.Data.Core.ServiceCollectionExtensions:70 — builds provider, sets AppHost.Current, runs Discover/Start)
VARIANT 2 — bare builder.Services.AddKoan(): S10.DevPortal, S14.AdapterBench, S8.Canon, S16.PantryPal MCP host; matches docs canon
VARIANT 3 — AddKoan() + fluent toggles + manual AppHost.Current ??= app.Services: S1.Web (.WithExceptionHandler().WithRateLimit()), g1c1.GardenCoop
VARIANT 4 — AddKoan().AsWebApi() (+ usually manual AppHost.Current ??=): the NEWEST samples — S18.Prism, S7.Meridian, S6.SnapVault, S8.PolyglotShop, S16.PantryPal API
VARIANT 6 — console with manual Koan.Core.Hosting.App.AppHost.Current = sp after BuildServiceProvider: S3.Mq.Sample

Code-verified redundancy: KoanWebOptions defaults are AutoUse=true, AutoMapControllers=true, EnableStaticFiles=true, EnableSecureHeaders=true, HealthPath=/api/health — and KoanWebStartupFilter.cs:31 sets AppHost.Current + runs Discover/Start itself (plus AppHostBinderHostedService as backstop). Therefore AsWebApi() merely re-asserts defaults + AddProblemDetails + exception handler, and every manual 'AppHost.Current ??= app.Services' in samples is dead weight — yet 8 of 13 in-sln samples carry one or both, teaching newcomers that the magic needs manual safety nets.
canonicalProgramCs: The canonical Program.cs per the newest docs is the 4-line form: `var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args); builder.Services.AddKoan(); var app = builder.Build(); app.Run();` — stated in docs/index.md:79-84 (last_updated 2026-03-23, the newest user-facing doc: 'This single line handles all the complexity'), docs/guides/building-apis.md:42 (2025-11-09: 'Bootstrap – Add Koan.Web and call services.AddKoan()'), and most explicitly in .claude/skills/bootstrap/SKILL.md ('services.AddKoan() is the ONLY line needed in Program.cs') and docs/examples/_canonical-samples.md ('Use these snippets across docs to keep examples consistent and drift-free'). Code verification confirms this form IS functionally sufficient when Koan.Web is referenced (startup filter auto-wires routing/controllers/health/AppHost). BUT it is stated authoritatively only in (a) a doc whose front-matter says `status: draft` (_canonical-samples.md), and (b) an AI-assistant skill file — neither is a normative ADR or a published guide a newcomer would find; no shipping sample uses the pure 4-line form (every web sample adds AsWebApi/AsProxiedApi/toggles/manual AppHost), and the live samples' modal pattern (AsWebApi + manual AppHost.Current) contradicts it. So: a canonical form exists, is correct, works, and is authoritatively stated nowhere a newcomer would believe, while the framework's own newest samples model a different, partially redundant idiom.
verdict: First-day verdict: the framework's onboarding is inverted — the deeper you get, the better it gets. The outermost ring (README, nuget.org) is actively hostile: every public install command is wrong (unprefixed package IDs resolve to a 13-months-stale 0.5.2 or NU1101; the real Sylin.* 0.8.0 prefix is documented only in internal ADRs; quickstart repos 404; badge/docs/skills all pin a never-published v0.6.3 while source is 0.17.x), and the README headlines at least three APIs that don't exist in the codebase (Flow pillar is an empty directory). The middle ring (getting-started, samples catalog) is 8 months stale, advertises four phantom samples as the 'Best Example' for entire pillars, hides five real ones, and contains zero instructions for the only path that works (clone + open Koan.sln). The innermost ring — S0→S1→S10→S14 ladder, the canonical 4-line Program.cs, KoanWebStartupFilter's genuinely complete auto-wiring — is real, runnable, and impressively low-ceremony, but is contradicted by its own exhibits: 5-7 bootstrap variants across samples, redundant AppHost/AsWebApi incantations in 8 of 13 in-sln samples, and a flagship (S5.Recs) that violates the 'never manually register framework services' doctrine in line 16 of its Program.cs. Concept economics: Koan roughly matches ASP.NET+EF's concept count for hello-CRUD at far fewer lines, and decisively beats it on integration volume for an AI/vector app — but 100% of its ~24 concepts are proprietary, documented only by this repo, and the repo's entry-level documentation is the least trustworthy layer in it. For a 10-month-old single-implementor framework this is a publishing/doc-drift problem, not an architecture problem: the single highest-leverage fixes are (1) publish current Sylin.* packages or delist/redirect the stale ones, (2) one README command that actually works, (3) delete or regenerate CATALOG.md from the sln, (4) promote the draft _canonical-samples.md Program.cs to normative and make every in-sln sample comply.
